relation_definitions.equivalent: Same meaning, interchangeable in any context.
relation_definitions.paraphrase: Same meaning, different register or formality, NOT interchangeable (e.g., 'ins Blaue hinein' vs. 'ohne klares Ziel').
relation_definitions.related: Shares semantic field or topic, but the meaning differs in a load-bearing way (e.g., 'Tomaten auf den Augen' vs. 'die Augen vor etwas verschließen').
relation_definitions.unrelated: No shared meaning; pairing is plausibly a 'false friend' or random pair from the planted tables.

attested_pair_flag_meaning: Boolean. 'true' for pairs that appear in backend/data/attested_pairs.json (Goethe/Schiller attested list from Phase 8.2); 'false' for the curated-phrases subset. The flag is binary and recorded per row.

row_schema.phrase_a_id: Slug FK into phrases.id. Lowercase-hyphenated, 3-120 chars.
row_schema.phrase_b_id: Slug FK into phrases.id. Same shape, must be distinct from phrase_a_id.
row_schema.phrase_a: Surface form of phrase A (5-200 chars).
row_schema.phrase_b: Surface form of phrase B (5-200 chars).
row_schema.relation: One of: equivalent, paraphrase, related, unrelated.
row_schema.attested_pair: Boolean. True only when (phrase_a_id, phrase_b_id) appears in backend/data/attested_pairs.json.
row_schema.rationale: Free-text one-sentence explanation of the relation choice (1-400 chars).
row_schema.source_attribution_a: One of the literal set {dwds, goethe, schiller, manual} (or comma-joined, e.g. 'dwds,goethe'). Pulled from the planted phrases row at labeling time.
row_schema.labeled_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of the labeling event.

hard_rules: Both phrase_a_id and phrase_b_id MUST exist in the planted phrases table (the labeler helper validates this at insert time).
Same pair (phrase_a_id, phrase_b_id) MUST NOT appear twice; the helper is idempotent on the pair key.
JSONL file MUST carry the leading 'provenance: HUMAN-LABELED' header. The card body and the Phase 1.5a discipline require this tag.
No LLM call in the labeler path. Human judgment only.
attested_pair is binary and recorded per row. The attested-pair shortcut pre-fills relation + attested_pair=true.
Distribution across the 4 relations MUST stay within ~12-13 per relation. The helper script warns after ~25 labels if any relation is under-represented.
phase_a_scaffolding_note: Phase A (this card's worker run) ships the empty JSONL + this manifest + the attested-pairs scaffold + the labeler helper script + the test suite. Phase B (Anurag's hand-label session) populates the JSONL to the 50-row target. Both phases share the same manifest schema; current_count and current_distribution are updated by the labeler helper after each accepted row.
